item.title: Re: Typo
item.content:encoded: Signing is intentional. -1's can't speak and communicate with sign language.

item.title: Typo
item.content:encoded: In the Colorado Springs event, it mentions the -1 instance signing when it seems like it should be singing.

item.title: Re: looking to comment section for clarity
item.content:encoded: 4953 doesn't know much about our world's culture, but he's not incapable of understanding ideas that don't involve fast food companies. And he understands how his universe's wars are conceptually tied to our universe's economy.

item.title: looking to comment section for clarity
item.content:encoded: I didn't understand what 4953 meant when it said, "Just as your kind squabbles with coins and numbers, we fight by shield and blade." Then you explained that the restaurant war correlates to franchise activities and the ideas clicked in my mind. By squabbles of coins and numbers he meant the competition to make money between franchises. Is 4953's understanding of our world limited to the operation of fast food companies?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
6th manifestation log added

happy april 1st hope this is a cool enough plant

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting SCP to be sure, goofy and zany like wondertainment stuff but just enough grounding(beef) its not too cartoonish to imagine. Really gives you are what you eat a new meaning.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah that makes sense. What I was going for with the ending is an implication that the events in restaurant war world are correlated with franchises in baseline reality.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's a concept called "mutual discovery", so it's entirely possible you had no idea 4486 existed. It's okay and it's not worth pressing, I don't want to detract any more from your hard work than I already may have by mentioning this.

What I mean by switching gears is that this piece is just zany zany zany in each part. I do not feel any high or low but rather a persistent theme of zany throughout. Adding Arbys doesn't change much aside from the implication of a franchise war. This is good foreshadowing, but unfortunately, the appearance of Arby's has meaning to The King, but not to me as a reader beyond whatever intrinsic feelings I happen to have about Arby's. Does this make sense?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope, this is the first time I've seen this pdf. My inspiration was actually this meme.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Were you inspired by Wendy's?

https://www.feastoflegends.com/images/Feast_Of_Legends.pdf

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It feels similar to 4486 at face value, but at the same time I think the tone and and specifics do a good job of differentiating the two. They feel like they have totally different goals as pieces, so beyond a similar premise I wouldn't really compare them.

I appreciate that you spun The King pretty much the same way he's spun in the actual commercials; eccentric and wacky, but ultimately fairly benevolent. Also I think a tale about the actual battle he's fighting would be hilarious. Enthusiastic +1, and I've never even had any BK food.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Let me guess, the tyrant's name is Trent.

The Tyrant Trent Arby's.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I hadn’t actually read 4486 (or knew it existed) till a few days before posting this.

I’m not sure I get how introducing Arby’s is an “inability to switch gears,” unless you meant that it’s a poor war to do it? Which I suppose is understandable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This feels rather derivative of SCP-4486 and I don't know how to feel about that…

Edit: Having re-read and thought about it more, I am a no-vote on this piece. It does a nice job on the zany and the imagery throughout is fairly effective. But, introduce Arbys at the end doesnt serve as an escalation rather a misdirection to a slightly better zany. Overall, this article's chief problem to me is an inability to switch gears. Still, fairly good work.

item.title: Great Job!
item.content:encoded: Great first article! Loved the draft while I was reading it. I knew many others would like it too. The characterization, the interactions, and the world building was awesome. Thank YOU for letting me read and help you out!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When I first started reading, I thought it was gonna end up as a downvote… but damn if this didn't keep me enthralled the whole way through. +1 for an excellent first article! I can't wait to see what you do next

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is awesome. As a burger lover, I especially like this concept. Well written, silly, and creative. I also like the implication of a “war” between the fast food chains. Good job.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I saw where the concept was going as I read the description, and the experiment logs didn't escalate very well or otherwise carry a narrative. I feel like the interview could be condensed and made more engaging, though the ending with the Arby's was sufficient for me to novote overall.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's good imagery here. However, the piece as a whole is too literal for my tastes. I could tell where this was headed from the beginning of the second paragraph, and it didn't surprise me by the end. That left me a little disappointed, but that's more a matter of personal preference than anything else.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this. Got exactly the right amount of silliness, taken and presented seriously, to work as a mainlister rather than a -J. Formatting and tone are great and the dialogue is fantastic.

+1 for sure.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
This is my first SCP! I hope y'all enjoy it.

Thank you to Zyn, cybersqyd, MalyceGraves, red3, Jak, and DrFullham for crit.
